sanskrit:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः।
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः॥
transliteration:
asaṃyatātmanā yogo duṣprāpa iti me matiḥ |
vaśyātmanā tu yatatā śakyo 'vāptum upāyataḥ ||
literal_meaning: By an uncontrolled mind, yoga is hard to attain—so is My view. But by a controlled mind, through striving with proper means, it is possible to achieve.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that self-mastery is impossible when the mind is scattered; disciplined effort using the right method makes success feasible.
psychological_state: Recognition that disciplined attention, not raw willpower, is the gateway to inner stability.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media while trying to meditate and wondering why peace never comes.
anchor_text: Still your mind; disciplined effort makes the goal reachable.
